name: Chocolate Damnation
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter with semisweet chocolate in a saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat and add sugar. Combine flour, baking powder, salt, and vanilla into the chocolate mixture.
2. Line a greased 15 x 10-inch jelly-roll pan with waxed paper. Pour batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake at 350°F for 12 to 15 minutes, or until the cake springs back when lightly touched. It should be soft, not crisp. Turn out onto a rack and cool.
3. Meanwhile, melt 12 oz semisweet chocolate with coffee in a double boiler over hot water. Remove from heat and stir in egg yolks until pale yellow. Return to chocolate mixture, stirring until smooth. Gradually add liqueur and cool.
4. Beat egg whites into stiff peaks. Whip cream into stiff peaks. Fold cream into the cooled chocolate mixture, then fold in egg whites.
5. Invert the brownie shell onto a towel and peel off paper. Line a greased 9-inch square baking dish with the brownie shell, cutting strips for sides and using the square for the bottom. Turn filling into the cake-lined dish and cover with plastic wrap. Chill for 3 to 4 hours or until firm.
6. Melt 4 oz semisweet chocolate with coffee in a double boiler over hot water.
7. Invert the chilled dessert onto a serving platter. Coat with chocolate glaze. Let the glaze set, then decorate with chocolate curls. Cut into very thin slices to serve.
